spec: Implement an IntervalSet class for inclusive integer intervals. State is a sorted, non-overlapping list of ranges. add(start,end) inserts an interval, swapping bounds if start>end, and merges any overlaps or touching intervals (e.g. [1,2] and [3,4] become [1,4]). remove(start,end) deletes all covered values, possibly splitting intervals; swapped bounds also apply. contains(x) returns True if x lies in any stored interval. ranges() returns a deep copy of the current intervals as [start,end] pairs in sorted order. Operations are order-dependent and must preserve invariants after every call.
test_code:
import pytest

from target_module import IntervalSet


def test_initial_state_contains_and_ranges_are_empty():
    s = IntervalSet()
    assert s.ranges() == []
    assert s.contains(0) is False
    assert s.contains(-10) is False
    assert s.contains(999) is False


def test_add_swaps_reversed_bounds_and_returns_sorted_range():
    s = IntervalSet()
    result = s.add(5, 2)
    assert result == [[2, 5]]
    assert s.ranges() == [[2, 5]]
    assert s.contains(2) is True
    assert s.contains(5) is True
    assert s.contains(1) is False
    assert s.contains(6) is False


def test_add_merges_overlapping_and_touching_intervals_across_multiple_ranges():
    s = IntervalSet()
    assert s.add(1, 2) == [[1, 2]]
    assert s.add(5, 6) == [[1, 2], [5, 6]]
    # Touches both existing intervals and should merge everything.
    assert s.add(3, 4) == [[1, 6]]
    assert s.ranges() == [[1, 6]]

    # Add an overlapping interval that expands the merged range.
    assert s.add(6, 10) == [[1, 10]]
    assert s.ranges() == [[1, 10]]


def test_add_inserts_before_and_after_without_merging_when_gap_exceeds_one():
    s = IntervalSet()
    s.add(10, 12)
    s.add(20, 22)

    # Insert strictly before first interval.
    assert s.add(1, 3) == [[1, 3], [10, 12], [20, 22]]

    # Insert strictly after last interval.
    assert s.add(30, 31) == [[1, 3], [10, 12], [20, 22], [30, 31]]

    # Insert in the middle with no touching.
    assert s.add(14, 15) == [[1, 3], [10, 12], [14, 15], [20, 22], [30, 31]]


def test_remove_swaps_bounds_and_can_split_interval_into_two():
    s = IntervalSet()
    s.add(1, 10)

    result = s.remove(7, 3)
    assert result == [[1, 2], [8, 10]]
    assert s.ranges() == [[1, 2], [8, 10]]

    assert s.contains(2) is True
    assert s.contains(3) is False
    assert s.contains(7) is False
    assert s.contains(8) is True


def test_remove_can_delete_whole_interval_trim_edges_and_ignore_disjoint_ranges():
    s = IntervalSet()
    s.add(1, 3)
    s.add(5, 7)
    s.add(10, 12)

    # Removing a disjoint range should leave state unchanged.
    assert s.remove(20, 30) == [[1, 3], [5, 7], [10, 12]]

    # Remove exact middle interval entirely.
    assert s.remove(5, 7) == [[1, 3], [10, 12]]

    # Trim left side of an interval.
    assert s.remove(0, 1) == [[2, 3], [10, 12]]

    # Trim right side of an interval.
    assert s.remove(12, 20) == [[2, 3], [10, 11]]


def test_contains_short_circuits_correctly_before_and_within_ranges():
    s = IntervalSet()
    s.add(5, 8)
    s.add(15, 18)

    assert s.contains(4) is False
    assert s.contains(5) is True
    assert s.contains(7) is True
    assert s.contains(9) is False
    assert s.contains(15) is True
    assert s.contains(19) is False


def test_ranges_returns_deep_copy_not_internal_state():
    s = IntervalSet()
    s.add(1, 2)
    s.add(5, 6)

    snapshot = s.ranges()
    snapshot.append([100, 200])
    snapshot[0][0] = -999

    assert snapshot == [[-999, 2], [5, 6], [100, 200]]
    assert s.ranges() == [[1, 2], [5, 6]]
    assert s.contains(1) is True
    assert s.contains(100) is False


def test_order_dependent_sequence_preserves_sorted_non_overlapping_invariants():
    s = IntervalSet()
    s.add(10, 12)
    s.add(1, 2)
    s.add(4, 4)
    s.add(3, 3)  # merges [1,2] and [4,4] through touching
    assert s.ranges() == [[1, 4], [10, 12]]

    s.remove(11, 11)
    assert s.ranges() == [[1, 4], [10, 10], [12, 12]]

    s.add(11, 11)
    assert s.ranges() == [[1, 4], [10, 12]]

    s.remove(-5, 100)
    assert s.ranges() == []

spec: Implement an IntervalSet class storing inclusive integer intervals, always kept sorted and non-overlapping. add(start,end) inserts an interval; if start>end, swap them. New intervals merge with any existing interval that overlaps or is directly adjacent (e.g. [1,2] with [3,4] -> [1,4]). remove(start,end) deletes covered values; if start>end, swap them. Removal may shrink or split intervals. contains(x) returns True if x lies in any interval. spans() returns a deep copy of the current interval list as [(start,end),...]. Mutations are order-dependent; invariants must hold after every operation.

import pytest

from target_module import IntervalSet


def test_initial_state_and_basic_contains_behavior():
    s = IntervalSet()
    assert s.spans() == []
    assert s.contains(0) is False
    assert s.contains(-10) is False


def test_add_sorts_reversed_bounds_and_merges_overlap_and_adjacency():
    s = IntervalSet()

    s.add(5, 3)  # reversed bounds should normalize to (3, 5)
    assert s.spans() == [(3, 5)]

    s.add(1, 2)  # adjacent to (3, 5), should merge
    assert s.spans() == [(1, 5)]

    s.add(7, 8)  # separate interval
    assert s.spans() == [(1, 5), (7, 8)]

    s.add(6, 6)  # bridges adjacency between both intervals
    assert s.spans() == [(1, 8)]


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "intervals, expected",
    [
        ([(10, 12), (1, 2), (5, 7)], [(1, 2), (5, 7), (10, 12)]),
        ([(1, 3), (5, 5), (4, 4)], [(1, 5)]),
        ([(2, 2), (4, 4), (3, 3)], [(2, 4)]),
    ],
)
def test_add_keeps_spans_sorted_non_overlapping_and_merges_when_needed(intervals, expected):
    s = IntervalSet()
    for start, end in intervals:
        s.add(start, end)
    assert s.spans() == expected

    spans = s.spans()
    for i in range(len(spans) - 1):
        a1, b1 = spans[i]
        a2, b2 = spans[i + 1]
        assert a1 <= b1
        assert a2 <= b2
        assert b1 + 1 < a2


def test_remove_no_overlap_partial_trim_split_and_reversed_bounds():
    s = IntervalSet()
    s.add(1, 10)
    s.add(20, 25)

    s.remove(30, 40)  # no overlap
    assert s.spans() == [(1, 10), (20, 25)]

    s.remove(1, 3)  # trim left edge
    assert s.spans() == [(4, 10), (20, 25)]

    s.remove(24, 25)  # trim right edge of second interval
    assert s.spans() == [(4, 10), (20, 23)]

    s.remove(6, 8)  # split first interval
    assert s.spans() == [(4, 5), (9, 10), (20, 23)]

    s.remove(22, 21)  # reversed bounds, removes 21..22
    assert s.spans() == [(4, 5), (9, 10), (20, 20), (23, 23)]

    s.add(1, 3)
    s.add(5, 7)
    s.add(9, 11)

    s.remove(2, 10)
    assert s.spans() == [(1, 1), (11, 11)]

    s.remove(-100, 100)
    assert s.spans() == []
    assert s.contains(1) is False
    assert s.contains(11) is False

    s = IntervalSet()

    s = IntervalSet()
    s.add(1, 3)
    s.add(10, 12)

    snapshot = s.spans()
    snapshot.append((100, 200))

def test_order_dependent_mutations_and_invariants_after_each_step():
    s = IntervalSet()

    s.add(5, 5)
    assert s.spans() == [(5, 5)]

    s.add(1, 2)

import pytest

def test_add_merges_overlapping_and_touching_intervals():
    s = IntervalSet()

    assert s.spans() == [(1, 3)]

    # Touching interval should merge with existing one.
    s.add(3, 5)
    assert s.spans() == [(1, 5)]

    # Disjoint interval remains separate.
    s.add(7, 9)
    assert s.spans() == [(1, 5), (7, 9)]

    # Adding an interval that bridges both should merge all into one.
    s.add(5, 7)
    assert s.spans() == [(1, 9)]

    assert s.spans() == [(2, 4), (6, 10)]

    assert s.spans() == [(2, 4), (6, 8)]

    s.remove(20, 30)

spec: Implement a RollingCounter class for integer timestamps. Construct with a positive window size. add(ts, value=1) records a count at timestamp ts; ts must be >= the most recent added ts, else raise ValueError. Multiple adds at the same ts accumulate. total() returns the sum of counts whose timestamps are in the inclusive range [latest_ts-window+1, latest_ts]. count_at(ts) returns the stored count exactly at ts if still retained, else 0. Old data must be discarded as new timestamps advance. If no events were added, total() and count_at(...) return 0.

import pytest

from target_module import RollingCounter


def test_init_requires_positive_window():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="window must be positive"):
        RollingCounter(0)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="window must be positive"):
        RollingCounter(-5)


def test_empty_counter_total_and_count_at_are_zero():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    assert rc.total() == 0
    assert rc.count_at(0) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(10) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(-1) == 0


def test_add_and_total_within_window_accumulates_values():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    assert rc.add(1) == 1
    assert rc.total() == 1
    assert rc.count_at(1) == 1

    assert rc.add(2, 4) == 5
    assert rc.total() == 5
    assert rc.count_at(1) == 1
    assert rc.count_at(2) == 4
    assert rc.count_at(3) == 0


def test_multiple_adds_same_timestamp_accumulate_in_single_bucket():
    rc = RollingCounter(5)
    assert rc.add(10, 2) == 2
    assert rc.add(10, 3) == 5
    assert rc.add(10) == 6

    assert rc.total() == 6
    assert rc.count_at(10) == 6
    assert rc.count_at(9) == 0


def test_add_rejects_decreasing_timestamps():
    rc = RollingCounter(4)
    rc.add(5, 2)
    rc.add(5, 1)
    rc.add(6, 3)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="timestamps must be non-decreasing"):
        rc.add(4, 10)

    # State should remain unchanged after the failed add.
    assert rc.total() == 6
    assert rc.count_at(5) == 3
    assert rc.count_at(6) == 3


def test_prunes_old_entries_when_timestamp_advances_past_window():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    rc.add(1, 2)
    rc.add(2, 3)
    rc.add(3, 4)
    assert rc.total() == 9

    # Latest becomes 4; cutoff is 2, so ts=1 should be pruned.
    assert rc.add(4, 5) == 12
    assert rc.total() == 12
    assert rc.count_at(1) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(2) == 3
    assert rc.count_at(3) == 4
    assert rc.count_at(4) == 5

    # Latest becomes 6; cutoff is 4, so ts=2 and ts=3 should be pruned.
    assert rc.add(6, 1) == 6
    assert rc.total() == 6
    assert rc.count_at(2) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(3) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(4) == 5
    assert rc.count_at(6) == 1


def test_window_of_one_keeps_only_latest_timestamp_even_with_gaps():
    rc = RollingCounter(1)
    assert rc.add(7, 2) == 2
    assert rc.total() == 2
    assert rc.count_at(7) == 2

    # Advancing to a new timestamp should drop the previous one immediately.
    assert rc.add(8, 5) == 5
    assert rc.total() == 5
    assert rc.count_at(7) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(8) == 5

    # Same timestamp still accumulates.
    assert rc.add(8, 1) == 6
    assert rc.total() == 6
    assert rc.count_at(8) == 6


def test_large_gap_prunes_everything_before_newest_entry():
    rc = RollingCounter(4)
    rc.add(1, 1)
    rc.add(2, 2)
    rc.add(3, 3)
    assert rc.total() == 6

    # Latest becomes 10; cutoff is 7, so all prior entries are removed.
    assert rc.add(10, 4) == 4
    assert rc.total() == 4
    assert rc.count_at(1) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(2) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(3) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(10) == 4


def test_count_at_returns_zero_for_missing_or_pruned_timestamp():
    rc = RollingCounter(2)
    rc.add(5, 7)
    assert rc.count_at(4) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(5) == 7

    rc.add(6, 1)
    assert rc.count_at(5) == 7
    assert rc.count_at(6) == 1

    # Move window so ts=5 is pruned; count_at should reflect pruning.
    rc.add(7, 2)
    assert rc.count_at(5) == 0
    assert rc.count_at(6) == 1
    assert rc.count_at(7) == 2
    assert rc.count_at(999) == 0

spec: Implement a RollingCounter class over integer event times with a fixed positive window size. add(t, value=1) records a non-negative integer value at time t. Times must be non-decreasing across add calls, else raise ValueError. total(now=None) returns the sum of values with timestamps in the inclusive range [end-window+1, end], where end is the last added time if now is None, otherwise now. If now is provided and is earlier than the last added time, raise ValueError. Empty structure totals to 0. Values outside the window are evicted as time advances; repeated totals must not change results.

import pytest

from target_module import RollingCounter


def test_init_validation_rejects_non_positive_or_non_int_window():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        RollingCounter(0)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        RollingCounter(-1)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        RollingCounter(1.5)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        RollingCounter("3")


def test_empty_total_and_total_none_before_any_add():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    assert rc.total() == 0
    assert rc.total(now=10) == 0
    assert rc.total(now=10) == 0


def test_add_and_total_basic_inclusive_window_behavior():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)

    assert rc.add(1) == 1
    assert rc.total() == 1

    assert rc.add(2, 2) == 3
    assert rc.total() == 3

    # Window at end=3 includes times 1, 2, 3
    assert rc.add(3, 4) == 7
    assert rc.total() == 7

    # Advancing to time 4 keeps times 2, 3, 4 and evicts time 1
    assert rc.total(now=4) == 6
    # Repeated totals should be stable
    assert rc.total(now=4) == 6

    rc = RollingCounter(2)

    assert rc.add(5, 0) == 0
    assert rc.total() == 0

    assert rc.add(5, 3) == 3
    assert rc.total() == 3

    # Zero-value add at a later time advances the effective end and triggers eviction
    assert rc.add(7, 0) == 0
    assert rc.total() == 0

    assert rc.add(10, 3) == 5

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):

    assert rc.total(now=5) == 3
    assert rc.total(now=5) == 3

    # Adding at a later time should include surviving and new values
    assert rc.add(5, 4) == 7

    # end=6 => cutoff=4, so time 3 is evicted, only time 5 remains

spec: Implement a RollingCounter class for per-key counts inside a sliding time window. Construct with a positive integer window size. add(ts,key,amount=1) records an event at integer timestamp ts with positive amount. Timestamps across all add/query calls must be non-decreasing; otherwise raise ValueError. total(ts,key=None) returns the sum of amounts with timestamp > ts-window and <= ts, either for one key or all keys if key is None. Old events must expire as time advances. Unknown keys return 0. Zero/negative window or amount raise ValueError.

import pytest

from target_module import RollingCounter


def test_init_valid_and_invalid_windows():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    assert rc.window == 3
    assert rc.total(0) == 0

    for bad in [0, -1, 1.5, "3", None, True]:
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            RollingCounter(bad)



def test_add_and_total_basic_per_key_and_all_keys():
    rc = RollingCounter(5)

    assert rc.add(1, "a") == 1
    assert rc.add(1, "a", 2) == 3
    assert rc.add(2, "b", 4) == 4

    assert rc.total(2, "a") == 3
    assert rc.total(2, "b") == 4
    assert rc.total(2) == 7
    assert rc.total(2, "missing") == 0



def test_window_expiration_boundary_is_exclusive_of_cutoff():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    rc.add(1, "a", 2)
    rc.add(3, "a", 1)
    rc.add(4, "b", 5)

    # At ts=4, window is (1, 4], so timestamp 1 should be expired.
    assert rc.total(4, "a") == 1
    assert rc.total(4, "b") == 5
    assert rc.total(4) == 6

    # At ts=6, window is (3, 6], so timestamp 3 should also be expired.
    assert rc.total(6, "a") == 0
    assert rc.total(6, "b") == 5
    assert rc.total(6) == 5



def test_expire_removes_keys_when_count_reaches_zero_and_unknown_key_returns_zero():

    rc.add(1, "x", 2)
    rc.add(2, "y", 3)

    assert rc.total(2, "x") == 2
    assert rc.total(2, "y") == 3

    # At ts=3, cutoff=1 so event at 1 expires and key x should disappear.
    assert rc.total(3, "x") == 0
    assert rc.total(3, "y") == 3
    assert rc.total(3, "nope") == 0

    # At ts=4, cutoff=2 so y also expires.
    assert rc.total(4) == 0
    assert rc.total(4, "y") == 0



def test_non_decreasing_timestamps_enforced_across_add_and_total_calls():
    rc = RollingCounter(5)
    rc.add(10, "a", 1)
    assert rc.total(10) == 1
    rc.add(10, "b", 2)  # equal timestamp is allowed
    assert rc.total(11) == 3

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        rc.add(10, "c", 1)  # less than last_ts=11

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        rc.total(9)



def test_invalid_timestamps_raise_for_add_and_total():
    rc = RollingCounter(4)

    for bad_ts in [1.2, "1", None, True]:
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            rc.add(bad_ts, "a")

    for bad_ts in [[], {}, False, 2.0]:
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            rc.total(bad_ts)



def test_invalid_amounts_raise_and_do_not_mutate_state():
    rc = RollingCounter(5)
    rc.add(1, "a", 2)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        rc.add(2, "a", 0)

    # _check_ts runs before amount validation, so last_ts advances to 2.
    assert rc.total(2, "a") == 2

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        rc.add(3, "a", -1)

    assert rc.total(3, "a") == 2
    assert rc.total(3) == 2



def test_query_only_usage_and_state_advances_with_totals():
    rc = RollingCounter(2)

    assert rc.total(1) == 0
    assert rc.total(1, "anything") == 0

    rc.add(1, "a", 1)
    assert rc.total(2) == 1

    # total advances last_ts, so going backwards should fail.
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        rc.add(1, "a", 1)

import pytest

from target_module import RollingWindowStats


def test_constructor_rejects_non_positive_capacity():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="capacity must be positive"):
        RollingWindowStats(0)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="capacity must be positive"):
        RollingWindowStats(-3)


def test_empty_window_methods_raise_and_values_is_empty():
    stats = RollingWindowStats(3)
    assert stats.values() == []
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="empty window"):
        stats.mean()
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="empty window"):
        stats.minmax()


def test_add_mean_minmax_and_values_without_eviction():
    stats = RollingWindowStats(5)

    assert stats.add(2) == 1
    assert stats.values() == [2]
    assert stats.mean() == pytest.approx(2.0)
    assert stats.minmax() == (2, 2)

    assert stats.add(4) == 2
    assert stats.add(6) == 3
    assert stats.values() == [2, 4, 6]
    assert stats.mean() == pytest.approx(4.0)
    assert stats.minmax() == (2, 6)


def test_eviction_keeps_only_most_recent_items_and_updates_stats():
    stats = RollingWindowStats(3)

    assert stats.add(10) == 1
    assert stats.add(20) == 2
    assert stats.add(30) == 3
    assert stats.values() == [10, 20, 30]
    assert stats.mean() == pytest.approx(20.0)
    assert stats.minmax() == (10, 30)

    # Evict 10
    assert stats.add(40) == 3
    assert stats.values() == [20, 30, 40]
    assert stats.mean() == pytest.approx(30.0)
    assert stats.minmax() == (20, 40)

    # Evict 20
    assert stats.add(5) == 3
    assert stats.values() == [30, 40, 5]
    assert stats.mean() == pytest.approx((30 + 40 + 5) / 3)
    assert stats.minmax() == (5, 40)


def test_capacity_one_always_keeps_latest_value():
    stats = RollingWindowStats(1)

    assert stats.add(7) == 1
    assert stats.values() == [7]
    assert stats.mean() == pytest.approx(7.0)
    assert stats.minmax() == (7, 7)

    assert stats.add(-2) == 1
    assert stats.values() == [-2]
    assert stats.mean() == pytest.approx(-2.0)
    assert stats.minmax() == (-2, -2)

    assert stats.add(100) == 1
    assert stats.values() == [100]
    assert stats.mean() == pytest.approx(100.0)
    assert stats.minmax() == (100, 100)


def test_minmax_exercises_both_low_and_high_update_branches():

    for value in [5, 3, 8, 2, 9, 2, 9]:

    assert stats.values() == [5, 3, 8, 2, 9, 2, 9]
    assert stats.minmax() == (2, 9)
    assert stats.mean() == pytest.approx(sum([5, 3, 8, 2, 9, 2, 9]) / 7)

    stats.add(2)

    snapshot.append(999)

def test_many_evictions_preserve_order_and_correct_running_stats():

    assert stats.mean() == pytest.approx((7 + 8 + 9 + 10) / 4)
    assert stats.minmax() == (7, 10)

spec: Implement a RollingStats class for a fixed-size sliding window of numbers. Constructor takes capacity>0. add(x) appends x; if full, it evicts the oldest value. mean() returns the arithmetic mean of current items, raising IndexError if empty. min() and max() return current minimum/maximum, also raising IndexError if empty. values() returns the current window contents from oldest to newest as a new list. clear() empties the window. All methods are order-dependent; duplicate values and negative numbers must be handled correctly.

import pytest

from target_module import RollingStats


def test_constructor_rejects_non_positive_capacity():

        RollingStats(0)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="capacity must be positive"):
        RollingStats(-3)


@pytest.mark.parametrize("method_name", ["mean", "min", "max"])
def test_empty_window_stat_methods_raise_index_error(method_name):
    rs = RollingStats(3)
    method = getattr(rs, method_name)
    with pytest.raises(IndexError, match="empty window"):
        method()

    assert rs.add(2) == 1
    assert rs.values() == [2]
    assert rs.mean() == pytest.approx(2.0)
    assert rs.min() == 2
    assert rs.max() == 2

    assert rs.add(4) == 2
    assert rs.add(6) == 3
    assert rs.values() == [2, 4, 6]
    assert rs.mean() == pytest.approx(4.0)
    assert rs.min() == 2
    assert rs.max() == 6



def test_add_evicts_oldest_when_capacity_reached_and_preserves_order():
    rs = RollingStats(3)

    assert rs.add(10) == 1
    assert rs.add(20) == 2
    assert rs.add(30) == 3
    assert rs.values() == [10, 20, 30]
    assert rs.mean() == pytest.approx(20.0)

    # Adding past capacity should evict the oldest item (10)
    assert rs.add(40) == 3
    assert rs.values() == [20, 30, 40]
    assert rs.mean() == pytest.approx((20 + 30 + 40) / 3)
    assert rs.min() == 20
    assert rs.max() == 40

    # Another eviction should remove 20 next
    assert rs.add(5) == 3
    assert rs.values() == [30, 40, 5]
    assert rs.mean() == pytest.approx((30 + 40 + 5) / 3)
    assert rs.min() == 5
    assert rs.max() == 40



def test_duplicates_and_negative_numbers_are_handled_correctly():

    assert rs.mean() == pytest.approx(1.0)
    assert rs.min() == -1
    assert rs.max() == 3

    # Evict one duplicate and add another negative value
    rs.add(-5)
    assert rs.values() == [-1, 3, 3, -5]
    assert rs.mean() == pytest.approx(0.0)
    assert rs.min() == -5
    assert rs.max() == 3



def test_values_returns_new_list_not_internal_storage():

spec: Implement class RollingCounter(window_size) for counting recent integer events. window_size must be > 0 or raise ValueError. State is order-sensitive. add(timestamp, value=1) appends an event; timestamps must be non-decreasing across adds or raise ValueError. value may be any int, including negative or zero. total(now=None) returns the sum of values whose timestamps are within the inclusive window [T-window_size+1, T], where T is the latest added timestamp if now is None, otherwise the provided now. query(now) is like total(now) but raises ValueError if now is less than the latest pruned/query time already used.

import pytest

from target_module import RollingCounter


def test_init_rejects_non_positive_window_sizes():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="window_size must be positive"):
        RollingCounter(0)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="window_size must be positive"):
        RollingCounter(-3)


def test_total_on_empty_counter_returns_zero_and_query_advances_time():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)

    assert rc.total() == 0
    assert rc.total(5) == 0
    assert rc.query(5) == 0

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="now cannot go backwards"):
        rc.total(4)


def test_add_and_total_with_default_now_and_inclusive_window_behavior():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)

    assert rc.add(1, 2) == 2
    assert rc.total() == 2

    assert rc.add(2, 3) == 5
    assert rc.total() == 5

    assert rc.add(3, 4) == 9
    assert rc.total() == 9

    # Window at T=4 is [2, 4], so timestamp 1 is pruned.
    assert rc.total(4) == 7

    # Window at T=5 is [3, 5], so timestamp 2 is also pruned.
    assert rc.total(5) == 4


def test_add_prunes_when_timestamp_advances_and_returns_current_sum():
    rc = RollingCounter(2)

    assert rc.add(1, 5) == 5
    assert rc.add(2, 7) == 12

    # Adding at timestamp 4 causes pruning with window [3, 4], removing prior events.
    assert rc.add(4, 1) == 1
    assert rc.total() == 1
    assert list(rc.events) == [(4, 1)]


def test_non_decreasing_timestamps_allow_equal_but_reject_decrease():
    rc = RollingCounter(5)

    assert rc.add(10, 1) == 1
    assert rc.add(10, 2) == 3
    assert rc.total() == 3

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="timestamps must be non-decreasing"):
        rc.add(9, 1)


def test_negative_and_zero_values_are_supported_and_affect_sum():
    rc = RollingCounter(4)

    assert rc.add(1, 5) == 5
    assert rc.add(2, 0) == 5
    assert rc.add(3, -2) == 3
    assert rc.total() == 3

    # At now=5, window is [2, 5], so timestamp 1 falls out.
    assert rc.total(5) == -2


def test_total_and_query_reject_backwards_time_after_pruning():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    rc.add(1, 1)
    rc.add(2, 2)

    assert rc.total(4) == 2

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="now cannot go backwards"):
        rc.query(3)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="now cannot go backwards"):
        rc.total(2)


def test_add_after_future_query_does_not_prune_backwards_but_total_still_works():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    rc.add(1, 10)
    rc.add(2, 20)

    # Advance internal last_time into the future.
    assert rc.query(10) == 0

    # Add at an equal-or-greater timestamp than last add, but less than last queried time.
    # This should not trigger pruning because timestamp <= _last_time.
    assert rc.add(3, 5) == 5

    # Querying at the existing last_time should keep only events in [8, 10], i.e. none.
    assert rc.total(10) == 0

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="now cannot go backwards"):
        rc.total(9)

spec: Implement a HitWindow class tracking integer timestamps in nondecreasing order. It is created with a positive window size. add(ts, count=1) records count hits at timestamp ts and returns the total hits currently within the inclusive range [ts-window+1, ts]. count(ts=None) returns hits in the current window; if ts is None, use the latest seen timestamp, or 0 if empty. Timestamps passed to add must not go backwards; otherwise raise ValueError. count(ts) must not mutate state and may query past or future times relative to latest timestamp.

import pytest

from target_module import HitWindow


def test_init_requires_positive_window():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="window must be positive"):
        HitWindow(0)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="window must be positive"):
        HitWindow(-3)


def test_empty_count_and_first_add_behavior():
    hw = HitWindow(3)

    assert hw.count() == 0
    assert hw.count(10) == 0

    returned = hw.add(5)
    assert returned == 1
    assert hw.count() == 1
    assert hw.count(5) == 1
    assert hw.count(4) == 0
    assert hw.count(6) == 1


def test_add_merges_same_timestamp_and_zero_count_does_not_insert():
    hw = HitWindow(5)

    assert hw.add(10, 2) == 2
    assert hw.add(10, 3) == 5
    assert hw.count() == 5
    assert hw.count(10) == 5

    # zero-count add should still be allowed and should not change totals
    assert hw.add(10, 0) == 5
    assert hw.count() == 5
    assert hw.count(10) == 5

    # advancing time with zero count should evict old entries based on the new ts
    assert hw.add(15, 0) == 0
    assert hw.count() == 0
    assert hw.count(10) == 5
    assert hw.count(15) == 0


def test_add_rejects_negative_count_and_decreasing_timestamp():
    hw = HitWindow(4)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="count must be nonnegative"):
        hw.add(1, -1)

    hw.add(3, 2)
    hw.add(3, 1)
    hw.add(4, 1)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="timestamps must be nondecreasing"):
        hw.add(2, 1)


def test_sliding_window_evicts_old_entries_on_add():
    hw = HitWindow(3)

    assert hw.add(1, 2) == 2          # window at ts=1 => [ -1, 1 ]
    assert hw.add(2, 1) == 3          # window at ts=2 => [ 0, 2 ]
    assert hw.add(3, 4) == 7          # window at ts=3 => [ 1, 3 ]
    assert hw.count() == 7

    # At ts=4, timestamp 1 is still included because range is [2,4]? No, 1 should evict.
    assert hw.add(4, 1) == 6          # entries at 2,3,4 remain => 1 + 4 + 1
    assert hw.count() == 6
    assert hw.count(4) == 6

    # At ts=6, only timestamps in [4,6] remain after eviction on add
    assert hw.add(6, 2) == 3          # entries at 4 and 6 => 1 + 2
    assert hw.count() == 3
    assert hw.count(3) == 4           # historical query over existing deque contents
    assert hw.count(6) == 3


def test_count_with_specific_timestamp_is_non_mutating_for_past_and_future_queries():
    hw = HitWindow(5)
    hw.add(10, 2)
    hw.add(12, 3)
    hw.add(14, 5)

    current_total = hw.count()
    assert current_total == 10

    # Past query should compute against current stored data without mutating state
    assert hw.count(12) == 5          # timestamps in [8,12] => 10 and 12
    assert hw.count() == current_total

    # Future query should also not mutate state
    assert hw.count(20) == 0          # timestamps in [16,20] => none
    assert hw.count() == current_total

    # Query overlapping only the latest entry
    assert hw.count(16) == 5          # timestamps in [12,16] => 12 and 14 => 8


def test_count_queries_respect_inclusive_bounds():
    hw = HitWindow(3)
    hw.add(5, 1)
    hw.add(6, 2)
    hw.add(7, 3)

    # For ts=7, inclusive window is [5,7]
    assert hw.count(7) == 6
    # For ts=6, inclusive window is [4,6]
    assert hw.count(6) == 3
    # For ts=5, inclusive window is [3,5]
    assert hw.count(5) == 1
    # For ts=8, inclusive window is [6,8]
    assert hw.count(8) == 5


def test_latest_only_advances_and_zero_count_updates_current_window_context():
    hw = HitWindow(2)

    hw.add(1, 4)
    assert hw.count() == 4

    # Same timestamp with zero count should not change total or latest-derived count
    hw.add(1, 0)
    assert hw.count() == 4

    # Move latest forward with zero count; this should evict timestamp 1 from current window [2,2]
    hw.add(2, 0)
    assert hw.count() == 4

    hw.add(3, 0)
    assert hw.count() == 0
    assert hw.count(1) == 4
    assert hw.count(2) == 0

spec: Implement a RollingCounter class for fixed-size time windows. `hit(ts, value=1)` records a non-negative integer value at integer timestamp `ts` and returns the current window sum after adding it. Timestamps for `hit` are non-decreasing; calling `hit` with smaller `ts` raises ValueError. `total(ts=None)` returns the sum of all values with timestamp in `[current-window+1, current]`, where `current` is the latest seen timestamp or the provided `ts`. Querying with `ts` smaller than latest seen raises ValueError. Window size is a positive integer. Expired items must be discarded as time advances.

import pytest

from target_module import RollingCounter


def test_init_rejects_invalid_window_values():
    invalid_values = [0, -1, 1.5, '3', None, True]
    for value in invalid_values:
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            RollingCounter(value)


def test_total_before_any_hits_is_zero_and_none_default_works():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    assert rc.total() == 0
    assert rc.total(None) == 0


def test_hit_and_total_basic_accumulation_within_window():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)

    assert rc.hit(1) == 1
    assert rc.total() == 1

    assert rc.hit(2, 2) == 3
    assert rc.total() == 3
    assert rc.total(2) == 3

    assert rc.hit(3, 4) == 7
    assert rc.total() == 7


def test_same_timestamp_hits_merge_and_zero_value_does_not_add_entry():
    rc = RollingCounter(5)

    assert rc.hit(10, 2) == 2
    assert list(rc._q) == [(10, 2)]

    assert rc.hit(10, 3) == 5
    assert list(rc._q) == [(10, 5)]

    assert rc.hit(10, 0) == 5
    assert list(rc._q) == [(10, 5)]
    assert rc.total() == 5


def test_window_expiration_discards_old_items_as_time_advances():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)

    rc.hit(1, 1)
    rc.hit(2, 2)
    rc.hit(3, 3)
    assert rc.total() == 6

    # At t=4, window is [2, 4], so timestamp 1 expires.
    assert rc.total(4) == 5
    assert list(rc._q) == [(2, 2), (3, 3)]

    # Adding at t=5 advances again; window is [3, 5], so timestamp 2 expires.
    assert rc.hit(5, 4) == 7
    assert list(rc._q) == [(3, 3), (5, 4)]
    assert rc.total() == 7

    # Advance far enough that everything expires.
    assert rc.total(8) == 4
    assert rc.total(9) == 0
    assert list(rc._q) == []


def test_hit_rejects_invalid_value_inputs():
    rc = RollingCounter(4)

    for bad_value in [-1, 1.2, '2', None, True]:
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            rc.hit(1, bad_value)


def test_non_integer_timestamps_raise_type_error_for_hit_and_total():
    rc = RollingCounter(2)

    for bad_ts in [1.5, '1', None]:
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            rc.hit(bad_ts)

    rc.hit(1, 1)
    for bad_ts in [2.5, '2', None]:
        if bad_ts is None:
            # total(None) is valid and means use latest timestamp.
            assert rc.total(None) == 1
        else:
            with pytest.raises(TypeError):
                rc.total(bad_ts)


def test_timestamps_moving_backwards_raise_value_error_for_hit_and_total():
    rc = RollingCounter(5)
    rc.hit(10, 1)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        rc.hit(9, 1)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        rc.total(9)

    # Equal timestamp is allowed.
    assert rc.hit(10, 2) == 3
    assert rc.total(10) == 3


def test_total_can_advance_time_without_new_hits_and_updates_latest_state():
    rc = RollingCounter(4)
    rc.hit(2, 5)
    rc.hit(4, 1)

    # At t=5, both timestamps 2 and 4 are still in window [2, 5].
    assert rc.total(5) == 6

    # After querying at t=5, querying earlier should now fail because latest advanced.
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        rc.total(4)

    # At t=6, window is [3, 6], so timestamp 2 expires.
    assert rc.total(6) == 1


def test_window_size_one_only_counts_current_timestamp():
    rc = RollingCounter(1)

    assert rc.hit(7, 2) == 2
    assert rc.total() == 2

    # Advancing to next timestamp expires previous value immediately.
    assert rc.total(8) == 0
    assert rc.hit(8, 3) == 3
    assert rc.total() == 3

spec: Implement class RollingCounter(window_size). It stores integer timestamped deltas in insertion order. window_size must be > 0 or raise ValueError. add(ts, delta=1) appends an event; timestamps must be non-decreasing relative to prior adds or raise ValueError. total_at(ts) returns sum of deltas for events with timestamp in [ts-window_size+1, ts]. Query ts may be any int, but must not be less than the last added timestamp or raise ValueError. Expired events should be discarded during queries/adds so state shrinks over time. reset() clears all state and returns None.

from target_module import RollingCounter


def test_init_requires_positive_window_size():

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="window_size must be > 0"):

    assert rc.add(2, 2) == 3
    assert rc.add(3, 3) == 6

    # At ts=3, window is [1, 3]
    assert rc.total_at(3) == 6

    # Querying forward prunes ts=1; window is [2, 4]
    assert rc.total_at(4) == 5

    # Querying further prunes ts=2; window is [3, 5]
    assert rc.total_at(5) == 3

    rc = RollingCounter(2)

    assert rc.add(10, 5) == 5
    assert rc.add(11, 7) == 12

    # Adding at 13 should prune timestamps < 12, removing both prior events.
    assert rc.add(13, 4) == 4
    assert rc.total_at(13) == 4
    assert rc.total_at(14) == 4

    rc = RollingCounter(5)

    assert rc.add(8, 2) == 2
    assert rc.add(8, 3) == 5  # equal timestamps are allowed
    assert rc.total_at(8) == 5

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="timestamps must be non-decreasing"):
        rc.add(7, 1)

    rc = RollingCounter(4)
    rc.add(5, 10)
    rc.add(6, 1)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="query timestamp too old"):
        rc.total_at(5)

    # Equal to last added timestamp is allowed.
    assert rc.total_at(6) == 11



def test_total_at_on_empty_counter_and_future_queries():
    rc = RollingCounter(10)

    assert rc.total_at(-100) == 0
    assert rc.total_at(0) == 0
    assert rc.total_at(100) == 0

    rc.add(3, 4)
    assert rc.total_at(20) == 0  # event should be pruned by far-future query



def test_reset_clears_state_and_allows_fresh_sequence():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    rc.add(1, 2)
    rc.add(2, 3)

    assert rc.reset() is None
    assert rc.total_at(0) == 0
    assert rc.total_at(100) == 0

    # After reset, old timestamp constraints should no longer apply.
    assert rc.add(-5, 7) == 7
    assert rc.total_at(-5) == 7



def test_negative_and_zero_deltas_are_counted_correctly():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)

    assert rc.add(1, 5) == 5
    assert rc.add(2, 0) == 5
    assert rc.add(3, -2) == 3
    assert rc.total_at(3) == 3

    # At ts=4, window is [2, 4], so ts=1 expires.
    assert rc.total_at(4) == -2



def test_progressive_queries_shrink_state_and_keep_results_consistent():
    rc = RollingCounter(3)
    rc.add(1, 1)
    rc.add(2, 2)
    rc.add(3, 3)

    assert rc.total_at(4) == 5  # keeps events at 2 and 3
    assert rc.total_at(4) == 5  # repeated same query should be stable
    assert rc.total_at(6) == 0  # all events expired by now

    # After pruning everything via query, a new add should behave from empty state.
    assert rc.add(6, 9) == 9
    assert rc.total_at(6) == 9

Methods: add(x) appends x and evicts the oldest item if over capacity, returning the current average as float, or None if empty. stats() returns a dict {"count", "sum", "min", "max", "avg"}; for an empty window use None for min/max/avg. snapshot() returns the current items from oldest to newest. Capacity <= 0 must raise ValueError at construction. State is order-dependent: evictions happen exactly when size exceeds capacity, and min/max must stay correct after evicting the previous extreme.

from target_module import RollingWindow


def test_init_rejects_non_positive_capacity():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        RollingWindow(0)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        RollingWindow(-1)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        RollingWindow(-100)


def test_empty_window_stats_and_snapshot():
    rw = RollingWindow(3)

    assert rw.snapshot() == []
    assert rw.stats() == {
        'count': 0,
        'sum': 0,
        'min': None,
        'max': None,
        'avg': None,
    }


def test_add_within_capacity_returns_running_average_and_preserves_order():
    rw = RollingWindow(3)

    assert rw.add(2) == pytest.approx(2.0)
    assert rw.snapshot() == [2]
    assert rw.stats() == {
        'count': 1,
        'sum': 2,
        'min': 2,
        'max': 2,
        'avg': 2.0,
    }

    assert rw.add(4) == pytest.approx(3.0)
    assert rw.snapshot() == [2, 4]
    assert rw.stats() == {
        'count': 2,
        'sum': 6,
        'min': 2,
        'max': 4,
        'avg': 3.0,
    }

    assert rw.add(6) == pytest.approx(4.0)
    assert rw.snapshot() == [2, 4, 6]
    assert rw.stats() == {
        'count': 3,

import pytest

from target_module import RollingWindow


def test_constructor_rejects_non_positive_capacity():

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="capacity must be positive"):
        RollingWindow(-3)


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "values, expected",
    [
        ([5], {"count": 1, "sum": 5, "min": 5, "max": 5, "avg": 5.0}),
        ([1, 2, 3], {"count": 3, "sum": 6, "min": 1, "max": 3, "avg": 2.0}),
        ([-2, 0, 4], {"count": 3, "sum": 2, "min": -2, "max": 4, "avg": 2 / 3}),
    ],
)
def test_stats_for_non_empty_window(values, expected):

    for idx, value in enumerate(values, start=1):
        assert rw.push(value) == idx
    assert rw.stats() == expected



def test_stats_returns_none_when_empty_and_after_draining():

    assert rw.stats() is None

    rw.push(10)
    rw.push(20)
    assert rw.pop_oldest() == 10
    assert rw.pop_oldest() == 20
    assert rw.stats() is None

    rw = RollingWindow(3)
    assert rw.push(1) == 1
    assert rw.push(2) == 2
    assert rw.push(3) == 3

    # Exceed capacity: oldest value 1 should be evicted.
    assert rw.push(4) == 3
    assert rw.stats() == {
        "count": 3,
        "sum": 9,
        "min": 2,
        "max": 4,
        "avg": 3.0,
    }

    # Exceed again to ensure rolling behavior continues.
    assert rw.push(-1) == 3
    assert rw.stats() == {
        "count": 3,
        "sum": 6,
        "min": -1,
        "max": 4,
        "avg": 2.0,
    }



def test_pop_oldest_raises_on_empty_and_updates_stats_incrementally():
    rw = RollingWindow(4)
    with pytest.raises(IndexError, match="pop from empty window"):
        rw.pop_oldest()

    for v in [4, 1, 7]:
        rw.push(v)

    assert rw.pop_oldest() == 4
    assert rw.stats() == {
        "count": 2,
        "sum": 8,
        "min": 1,
        "max": 7,
        "avg": 4.0,
    }

    assert rw.pop_oldest() == 1
    assert rw.stats() == {
        "count": 1,
        "sum": 7,
        "min": 7,
        "max": 7,
        "avg": 7.0,
    }



def test_duplicate_values_are_tracked_correctly_in_min_and_max_queues():

    assert rw.stats() == {
        "count": 3,
        "sum": 6,
        "min": 2,
        "max": 2,
        "avg": 2.0,
    }

    # Removing one duplicate should keep min/max unchanged because duplicates remain.
    assert rw.pop_oldest() == 2
    assert rw.stats() == {
        "count": 2,
        "sum": 4,
        "min": 2,
        "max": 2,
        "avg": 2.0,
    }

    # Pushing another duplicate keeps the same extrema and count at capacity.
    assert rw.push(2) == 3
    assert rw.stats() == {
        "count": 3,
        "sum": 6,
        "min": 2,
        "max": 2,
        "avg": 2.0,
    }



def test_order_dependent_sequence_of_pushes_and_pops_matches_current_window_only():
    rw = RollingWindow(3)

    assert rw.push(3) == 1
    assert rw.stats() == {"count": 1, "sum": 3, "min": 3, "max": 3, "avg": 3.0}

    assert rw.push(1) == 2
    assert rw.stats() == {"count": 2, "sum": 4, "min": 1, "max": 3, "avg": 2.0}

    assert rw.stats() == {"count": 3, "sum": 8, "min": 1, "max": 5, "avg": 8 / 3}

    # Remove oldest: window becomes [5, 2]

    assert rw.stats() == {"count": 2, "sum": 7, "min": 2, "max": 5, "avg": 3.5}

    assert rw.push(-4) == 3
    assert rw.stats() == {"count": 3, "sum": 3, "min": -4, "max": 5, "avg": 1.0}

    # Capacity exceeded again: window becomes [2, -4, 6]
    assert rw.push(6) == 3
    assert rw.stats() == {"count": 3, "sum": 4, "min": -4, "max": 6, "avg": 4 / 3}



def test_capacity_one_always_keeps_only_latest_value():
    rw = RollingWindow(1)

    assert rw.push(9) == 1
    assert rw.stats() == {"count": 1, "sum": 9, "min": 9, "max": 9, "avg": 9.0}

    assert rw.pop_oldest() == -2

spec: Implement a WindowCounter class for rolling sums by integer timestamps. Construct with positive window size. add(ts, value) requires ts >= last added ts, else raises ValueError; it stores the event and returns current sum of values with timestamps in [ts-window+1, ts]. total(ts=None) returns the sum in the active window at the latest timestamp, or as of a provided ts using the same inclusive range; querying with ts before the latest added timestamp raises ValueError. Expire old events on add/total. Empty state totals to 0. Negative values are allowed.

import pytest

from target_module import WindowCounter


def test_init_requires_positive_window():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="window must be positive"):
        WindowCounter(0)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="window must be positive"):
        WindowCounter(-3)


def test_total_on_empty_counter_returns_zero_for_none_and_explicit_ts():
    wc = WindowCounter(3)
    assert wc.total() == 0
    assert wc.total(10) == 0
    assert wc.total(0) == 0


def test_add_and_total_basic_rolling_behavior_with_inclusive_window():
    wc = WindowCounter(3)

    assert wc.add(1, 5) == 5          # window [ -1, 1 ] contains ts=1
    assert wc.add(2, 7) == 12         # window [ 0, 2 ] contains ts=1,2
    assert wc.add(3, 1) == 13         # window [ 1, 3 ] contains ts=1,2,3
    assert wc.total() == 13

    # Advancing time by add should expire ts=1 for window [2,4]
    assert wc.add(4, 10) == 18        # 7 + 1 + 10
    assert wc.total() == 18

    # Querying a future timestamp should expire more without adding anything
    assert wc.total(5) == 11          # window [3,5] contains ts=3,4 => 1+10
    assert wc.total(6) == 10          # window [4,6] contains only ts=4


def test_same_timestamp_events_are_allowed_and_counted():
    wc = WindowCounter(2)

    assert wc.add(5, 2) == 2
    assert wc.add(5, 3) == 5
    assert wc.add(6, 4) == 9          # window [5,6] includes both ts=5 events and ts=6
    assert wc.total() == 9

    # At ts=7, window [6,7] should expire both ts=5 events together
    assert wc.total(7) == 4


def test_negative_values_supported_and_affect_sum_and_expiration():
    wc = WindowCounter(2)

    assert wc.add(1, 10) == 10
    assert wc.add(2, -3) == 7
    assert wc.add(3, -4) == -7        # window [2,3] => -3 + -4, ts=1 expired
    assert wc.total() == -7
    assert wc.total(4) == -4          # window [3,4] => only ts=3
    assert wc.total(5) == 0           # window [4,5] => all expired


def test_add_rejects_out_of_order_timestamps():
    wc = WindowCounter(3)
    assert wc.add(10, 1) == 1
    assert wc.add(10, 2) == 3  # equal timestamp is valid
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="timestamp out of order"):
        wc.add(9, 5)


def test_total_rejects_querying_past_state_after_adds():
    wc = WindowCounter(4)
    wc.add(3, 5)
    wc.add(6, 2)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="cannot query past state"):
        wc.total(5)



def test_total_future_query_mutates_state_and_later_past_query_fails():
    wc = WindowCounter(3)
    wc.add(1, 4)
    wc.add(2, 6)

    # Future query expires all old events by ts=10
    assert wc.total(10) == 0

    # latest_ts remains the latest added timestamp, so querying before that still fails
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="cannot query past state"):
        wc.total(1)

    # Adding at a later timestamp remains valid and starts from current expired state
    assert wc.add(10, 5) == 5
    assert wc.total() == 5


def test_window_size_one_keeps_only_current_timestamp():
    wc = WindowCounter(1)

    assert wc.add(1, 8) == 8
    assert wc.total() == 8
    assert wc.add(2, 1) == 1          # ts=1 expires immediately when ts=2 is added
    assert wc.add(2, 4) == 5          # same timestamp remains in current window
    assert wc.total(3) == 0           # window [3,3] excludes ts=2

spec: Implement a WindowCounter class that tracks integer events in a fixed-capacity rolling window of the most recent values. WindowCounter(capacity) requires capacity>0. add(x) appends x and evicts the oldest item if size would exceed capacity, returning the current window sum. total() returns the current sum. average() returns the arithmetic mean as float, or raises ValueError if empty. last(n) returns a list of the most recent n items in order from oldest to newest among those returned; if n<0 raise ValueError; if n exceeds size, return the whole window.

    assert wc.total() == 25
    assert wc.last(2) == [20, 5]
    assert wc.last(5) == [20, 5]

    # Exceeds again, so 20 should be evicted

    assert wc.total() == 0

    for value in [1, 2, 3]:

    assert wc.last(1) == [3]
    assert wc.last(2) == [2, 3]
    assert wc.last(10) == [1, 2, 3]

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="n must be non-negative"):

spec: Implement a WindowCounter class tracking integer events in the last N ticks. Constructor WindowCounter(window) requires window>0. Method add(value=1) records one event at the current tick, increasing that tick’s count by value; negative values raise ValueError. Method advance(steps=1) moves time forward by positive integer steps, expiring old ticks; returns current total in window. Method total() returns the sum for ticks in [current-window+1,current]. Calling add multiple times before advancing accumulates on the same tick. Advancing by many steps may clear all history.

import pytest

from target_module import WindowCounter


def test_init_valid_sets_initial_state():
    wc = WindowCounter(3)
    assert wc.window == 3
    assert wc.tick == 0
    assert wc.total_sum == 0
    assert wc.buckets == {}
    assert wc.total() == 0


@pytest.mark.parametrize("window", [0, -1, 1.5, "3", None, True])
def test_init_invalid_window_raises(window):
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        WindowCounter(window)


def test_add_default_and_multiple_adds_same_tick_accumulate():
    wc = WindowCounter(5)
    assert wc.add() == 1
    assert wc.add(2) == 3
    assert wc.add(0) == 3
    assert wc.total() == 3
    assert wc.buckets == {0: 3}


@pytest.mark.parametrize("value", [-1, 1.2, "2", None, False])
def test_add_invalid_value_raises(value):
    wc = WindowCounter(3)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        wc.add(value)


def test_advance_moves_time_and_prunes_outside_window_progressively():
    wc = WindowCounter(3)
    wc.add(2)          # tick 0 => total 2
    assert wc.advance() == 2   # tick 1, keep tick 0
    wc.add(3)          # tick 1 => total 5
    assert wc.advance() == 5   # tick 2, keep ticks 0..2
    wc.add(4)          # tick 2 => total 9
    assert wc.total() == 9

    # Move to tick 3: window covers 1..3, so tick 0 expires
    assert wc.advance() == 7
    assert wc.total() == 7
    assert 0 not in wc.buckets
    assert wc.buckets == {1: 3, 2: 4}

    # Move to tick 4: window covers 2..4, so tick 1 expires
    assert wc.advance() == 4
    assert wc.buckets == {2: 4}


@pytest.mark.parametrize("steps", [0, -2, 2.5, "1", None, False])
def test_advance_invalid_steps_raises(steps):
    wc = WindowCounter(2)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        wc.advance(steps)


def test_advance_many_steps_can_clear_all_history():
    wc = WindowCounter(4)
    wc.add(5)
    wc.advance()
    wc.add(6)
    assert wc.total() == 11

    # Jump far enough that all prior ticks are stale
    assert wc.advance(10) == 0
    assert wc.total() == 0
    assert wc.buckets == {}


def test_total_prunes_without_needing_explicit_advance_side_effects_visible():
    wc = WindowCounter(2)
    wc.add(1)          # tick 0
    wc.advance()       # tick 1
    wc.add(2)          # tick 1
    wc.advance()       # tick 2, tick 0 should prune here, total 2
    assert wc.total() == 2
    assert wc.buckets == {1: 2}


def test_window_of_one_only_keeps_current_tick():
    wc = WindowCounter(1)
    assert wc.add(2) == 2
    assert wc.add(3) == 5
    assert wc.total() == 5

    # Advancing one tick drops all previous data for window size 1
    assert wc.advance() == 0
    assert wc.total() == 0
    assert wc.buckets == {}

    # New additions at current tick are counted normally
    assert wc.add(4) == 4
    assert wc.buckets == {1: 4}
